title: Draft ≤10 source definitions (held in review, not published)
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual builds patient-facing oncology glossary entries requiring riskTier HIGH review. Before clinical and advocate reviewers are engaged (M1), a small set of draft definitions can be authored as a proof-of-concept, held in review status only, never published. This demonstrates the drafting pipeline and surfaces agent uncertainty flags. The BLOCKING license-000 prerequisite must pass before drafting begins.
objective: Draft at most 10 plain-language oncology/clinical-trial definitions sourced from allow-listed, license-confirmed sources, each at or below grade-8 reading level with notMedicalAdvice:true and sourceRefs. Emit UNCERTAIN: flags for anything ambiguous. Hold all entries in review status (unpublished); no sign-off is recorded because no reviewer is engaged yet.
acceptanceCriteria: license-000 has passed (derivative permission confirmed for seed sources) before drafting begins

At most 10 source definitions are drafted in glossary/source/<term-id>.yaml, each with: termId, term, definition (at or below grade 8), notMedicalAdvice:true, sourceRefs (pointing to allow-listed source), safetyLoad classification, and readingLevelScore
Agent uncertainty flags in the format UNCERTAIN: <termId> | <type> | <note> are captured in the review artifact as agentFlags for each entry
Any advice, dosing, diagnostic, or prognostic content is refused and flagged, never authored
All entries have reviewStatus: review (not published, not delivered, not done)
No clinicalSignoff or advocateSignoff is recorded (reviewers not yet engaged; these are drafts only)
Each definition cites only allow-listed sources confirmed in license-000
resources: PLAN.md — Solution approach section (Stage A pipeline)
output: glossary/source/<term-id>.yaml files (at most 10 entries) in review status with agentFlags captured; a companion review artifact per entry
